title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Proxy Trust, and Client Identity · Type Forensics
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken request authority and trusted-proxy decision record; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in request authority and trusted-proxy decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Interpret method, url, originalUrl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics.
Validate canonical host and port against configured authority before routing, link generation, cache keys, redirects, or tenant selection.
Configure trust proxy by explicit addresses or hop logic and derive client, protocol, and host only when every trusted hop overwrites forwarded fields.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Express Request Field Meaning
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 1906 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive authority only across an explicit trusted-proxy and host allow-list boundary; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then replace an average latency claim with p95 and event-loop saturation evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Interpret method, url, originalUrl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record bounded route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, personal data, or unbounded query values.

hints: For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Authority Redaction Evidence, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Authority Redaction Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Authority Redaction Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug team handles case 5848 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive authority only across an explicit trusted-proxy and host allow-list boundary; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record bounded route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, personal data, or unbounded query values.

For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Authority Redaction Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Logging originalUrl is safe because secrets appear only in authorization headers.
